test_cases.description: Verify that the application fetches all user records successfully when the JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns a complete response.
test_cases.name: Successful fetch of all users from JSONPlaceholder

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is accessible and returns a valid list of user objects at /users.
test_setup.then: Logging shows start of fetch, successful retrieval, correct count of users, and logs of each user summary. No errors are reported.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify that the application properly reports a failure and does not proceed when the JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable.
test_cases.name: API unreachable scenario

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is down or network is unavailable.
test_setup.then: An error is logged indicating the API is unreachable, and no further processing or user summary logging occurs.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify application behavior when the JSONPlaceholder API returns an empty user list.
test_cases.name: API returns empty user list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is accessible but returns an empty list at /users.
test_setup.then: Logging shows start of fetch, zero users fetched, and does not attempt to log any user summaries. No errors are reported.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles unexpected exceptions thrown by UserService gracefully.
test_cases.name: Internal error in UserService during fetch

test_setup.given: UserService.get_all_users() raises an exception (e.g., parsing error, unexpected response).
test_setup.then: An error is logged with details of the exception. No user summaries are logged.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify application behavior when the API returns malformed or invalid JSON.
test_cases.name: API returns invalid JSON

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint returns a non-JSON or malformed JSON response.
test_setup.then: An error is logged indicating a JSON parsing failure. No user summaries are logged.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Ensure that main() is not triggered when the script is imported as a module rather than run directly.
test_cases.name: Script imported as a module, main not executed

test_setup.given: src/main.py is imported from another module and not run as __main__.
test_setup.then: main() is not called. No logs related to user fetch or summaries are produced.

test_cases.description: Test how the application handles fetching and logging a very large user dataset.
test_cases.name: API returns a large number of users

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns a list of 1000+ user records at /users.
test_setup.then: Logging shows start, correct user count, and logs summaries for all users without error.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Check that the application does not crash and logs user summaries even if some user records have missing or null fields.
test_cases.name: API returns users with missing or null fields

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns user records with some fields missing or null (e.g., missing email or username).
test_setup.then: User summaries are logged; missing fields are handled gracefully without exceptions.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify that the application logs and handles HTTP error responses from /users endpoint.
test_cases.name: API returns HTTP error (e.g., 404, 500)

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint returns HTTP 500 error.
test_setup.then: An error is logged with the HTTP status code, and no user summaries are produced.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify application handles cases where /users returns a valid JSON object instead of a list.
test_cases.name: API returns valid JSON that is not a list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint returns a valid JSON object, not a list.
test_setup.then: An error or warning is logged indicating unexpected response format. No user summaries are logged.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify the function returns the complete list of users when the JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns a valid response.
test_cases.name: Fetch all users successfully

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and returns a valid user list containing 10 user records.
test_setup.then: The function returns a list of 10 User domain model objects that exactly match the response from the API, with no filtering, sorting, or transformation.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function handles the case where the JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable, such as due to network errors or invalid endpoint.
test_cases.name: API unreachable error handling

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable (e.g., network failure, DNS error, or endpoint returns timeout).
test_setup.then: The function raises a connection-related exception or returns an error indicating the API cannot be reached and does not proceed with further processing.

test_cases.description: Verify the function handles non-200 HTTP status codes from the API gracefully.
test_cases.name: API returns HTTP error

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.
test_setup.then: The function raises an exception or returns an error indicating the API returned an error response, and does not process any user records.

test_cases.description: Check the function's handling when the API returns an empty user list.
test_cases.name: API returns empty user list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns an empty list (no users).
test_setup.then: The function returns an empty list.

test_cases.description: Verify the function handles a malformed JSON response from the API.
test_cases.name: API returns malformed JSON

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available but returns a malformed JSON response (e.g., incomplete object, invalid syntax).
test_setup.then: The function raises a JSON parsing error exception or returns an error indicating invalid/malformed JSON.

test_cases.description: Test the function's ability to handle large datasets efficiently and without errors.
test_cases.name: API returns a large list of users

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns a list of 10000 user records.
test_setup.then: The function returns a list of 10000 User domain model objects matching the API response.

test_cases.description: Check the function when the API returns exactly one user object in a list.
test_cases.name: API returns a single user

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns a list with a single user record.
test_setup.then: The function returns a list containing exactly one User domain model matching the API response.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function's handling of user records with missing fields from the API.
test_cases.name: API returns incomplete user data

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns a list of users where some user objects are missing required fields (e.g., 'id' or 'name').
test_setup.then: The function raises a data validation exception or returns user objects with missing/None fields, depending on implementation.

test_cases.description: Test the function if the API returns a valid JSON object instead of a list.
test_cases.name: API returns non-array JSON root

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available but returns a JSON object (not a list/array) as the root response.
test_setup.then: The function raises a data type exception or returns an error indicating unexpected response format.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function does not filter, sort, or transform the user data received from the API.
test_cases.name: No modification of fetched user data

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns a user list with users in a specific order and with exact original field values.
test_setup.then: The function returns the list in the same order and with the same values as received from the API (i.e., no alteration).

test_cases.description: Verify that all user records are retrieved successfully when the JSONPlaceholder API is accessible and returns a valid response.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_success

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and /users endpoint responds with a valid user list.
test_setup.then: Returns a list of User domain models with all user records from the API.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function handles the scenario where the JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable (e.g., network failure, DNS failure, timeout) and reports the error.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_api_unreachable

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable due to network error.
test_setup.then: Function raises an appropriate error or exception indicating the API is unreachable.

test_cases.description: Check that the function raises an error when the API responds with a non-2xx HTTP status code (e.g., 404, 500).
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_http_error

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.
test_setup.then: Function raises an HTTP error indicating the request failed.

test_cases.description: Verify that the function returns an empty list when the API returns an empty user list.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_empty_list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with an empty list [].
test_setup.then: Returns an empty list.

test_cases.description: Ensure that the function raises an error when the API returns invalid (non-JSON) response.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_invalid_json_response

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with non-JSON or malformed JSON data.
test_setup.then: Function raises a JSON parse error.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function raises an error if a user record in the response cannot be parsed into the User domain model due to missing required fields.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_invalid_user_object

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with a list containing a user object missing required fields.
test_setup.then: Function raises a parsing or validation error for the invalid user object.

test_cases.description: Validate that if the API returns a mix of valid and invalid user objects, the function fails with an error and does not return a partial list.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_partial_user_records

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with a list containing valid and invalid user objects.
test_setup.then: Function raises an error rather than returning a partial list.

test_cases.description: Ensure that the function returns user records exactly as received from the API, with no filtering, sorting, or transformation.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_no_filtering_or_sorting

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint returns a list of user objects in a specific order.
test_setup.then: Returns user records in the same order and with the same data as the API response.

test_cases.description: Test the function's ability to handle large responses by simulating a large list of user objects.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_large_number_of_users

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with a list of 1000 user objects.
test_setup.then: Returns a list of 1000 User domain models.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function handles slow API responses gracefully, either by waiting for the response or timing out as configured.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_slow_response

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds after a significant delay.
test_setup.then: Function either returns the expected list after waiting or raises a timeout error if one is configured.
